rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Trifurcation Of Certain Issues At Trial/Bifurication Of Trial

rulings.Case Title: MOHAMMED AL-QARAWI, BY AND THROUGH HIS GUARDIAN AD VS. DOMINICAN UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, July 18, 2013, Line 23, DEFENDANTs BERLITZ LANDGUAGES, INC. [CASE NO. CGC-11-513062], ELS EDUCATIONAL SERVICES, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendants' ELS Educational Services, Inc. ("ELS") and Berlitz Languages, Inc. ("Berlitz") Motion for Summary Judgment as to the first cause of action in Plaintiff Al-Qarawi's Complaint (Case No. 510757) and Plaintiff Escobar's First Amended Complaint (Case No. 513062) is denied. Defendants met their initial burden by producing prima facie evidence that they did not control the manner and means by which Co-Defendants OEC International Traveler's Club, LLC ("OEC") and Huber performed their services. Millsap v. Fed. Express Corp., 227 Cal. App. 3d 425, 431 (Ct. App. 1991.) However, Plaintiffs and Co-Defendants met their shifted burden of production, and established that triable issues of fact exist whether Defendants ELS and Berlitz controlled the manner and means of OEC and Huber's services. A triable issue of fact exists whether ELS and Berlitz supplied vehicles to OEC to transport ELS students to and from the airport, and on overnight filed trips. (See Declaration of Akiko Kong in Support of Defendant OEC International Traveler's Club's Opposition to Defendants' Motion of Summary Judgment, at 1, 3.) Moreover, a triable issue of fact exits whether the ELS and Berlitz controlled the manner and means by which OEC transported students from the airport. (See OEC International Traveler's Club, LLC's Evidence in Support of Opposition for Motion for Summary Judgment, Exh. C, Tr. 64:2-65:5.) Lastly, a triable issue of fact exits whether ELS and Berltiz controlled the manner and means by which OEC performed its services for the overnight field trips ELS provided to its students. (See OEC International Traveler's Club, LLC's Evidence in Opposition for Motion for Summary Judgment, Exh. B, Tr. 29:15-24; Declaration of Huber in Support of Opposition to Defendant's Berlitz Languages, Inc. and ELS Educational Services, Inc. Motion for Summary Judgment or in the alternative, Summary Adjudication, at 13.) Because several inferences may be drawn about the control ELS and Berlitz had over the manner and means of OEC's service, ELS and Berlitz are not entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. (See Millsap v. Fed. Express Corp., 227 Cal. App. 3d 425, 431 (Ct. App. 1991).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TINA MARIE ENGLE VS. BOARD OF REGISTERED NURSING
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, July 18, 2013, Line 6, PETITIONER TINA ENGLE's Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate. Denied. (CCP § 1094.5.) Respondent Board of Registered Nursing did not abuse its discretion in revoking Petitioner's license. On December 2, 2012, Petitioner was convicted of grand theft pursuant to Penal Code § 487(a) for stealing a medical monitoring device. (Admin. Record, p. AR0004.) "The board may take disciplinary action against a certified or licensed nurse or deny an application for a certificate or license for . . . [c]onviction of a felony or of any offense substantially related to the qualifications, functions, and duties of a registered nurse, in which event the record of the conviction shall be conclusive evidence thereof." (Bus. & Prof. Code § 2761(f).) Petitioner's conviction for stealing a medical device related to investigations into a patient's death is substantially related to Petitioner's related to the qualifications, functions, and duties of a registered nurse. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WENDY OAKES VS. CELTIC MOVING & STORAGE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, July 18, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT CELTIC MOVING & STORAGE, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted, with 10 days leave to amend. Ms. Oakes has not pled facts to show malice, oppression or fraud to support her exemplary damages claim under Pub. Util. Code §, 5142(h). (Civ. Code § 3294(c).) Ms. Oakes is granted leave to amend, only if she can do so good in faith, to allege facts to show that Celtic Moving & Storage Inc. acted with the requisite malice, oppression or fraud. rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE R SCHEMPP VS. INTERNATIONAL ALLIANCE OF THEATRICAL STAGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Ofmotion Anmd Motion To Compel Deft'S Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Tanglible Things, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 18, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF STEPHAINE SCHEMPP Notice Ofmotion Anmd Motion To Compel Deft'S Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Tanglible Things, Set One Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance required. Parties are to meet and confer further before the hearing on July 18 and attempt to resolve issues as to those discovery requests that are still outstanding, and be prepared at the hearing to advise the Court of precisely which discovery requests are still at issue. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: GCFS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. CHRIS DESOTO AKA CHRISTOPHER DESOTO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Facts And Documents Deemed Admitted And For Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 18, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFF GCFS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Facts And Documents Deemed Admitted And For Attorney Fees Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant, unopposed. Sanctions imposed in amount of $430. Court will sign proposed order submitted by moving party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: LINDA DAVIDSON VS. MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, July 18, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued to August 28, 2013 to give Mercedes-Benz USA, LLC the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B and 2.6C. Mercedes-Benz USA, LLC must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers and operative complaint to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 18, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the demurrer will be overruled. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BONNIE LAU VS. DELOITTE TAX LLP, A LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas Of Employment Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, July 18, 2013, Line 16, PLAINTIFF BONNIE LAU's Motion To Quash Subpoenas Of Employment Records. Denied. Plaintiff's employment records from four prior employers are discoverable as Plaintiff has waived her right to privacy by bringing this employment action against Defendant. Further, these records are relevant to establishing Plaintiff's ability to mitigate her loss from employment termination. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
